Information Retrieval Techniques Course Schedule
A rough schedule for the course, outlining weekly topics and important dates, subject to change.

data.entity_name: Information Retrieval Techniques Course Boolean Retrieval
data.content:
Information Retrieval Techniques Course Boolean Retrieval
A topic covering the fundamentals of Boolean retrieval, part of Week 1's content.

Information Retrieval Techniques Course BM25
A topic discussing the BM25 ranking function, covered

data.entity_name: Information Retrieval Techniques Course Course Overview
data.content:
Information Retrieval Techniques Course Course Overview
An overview of the course content and structure for the first week.

data.entity_name: Information Retrieval Techniques Course Vector Space Model
data.content:
Information Retrieval Techniques Course Vector Space Model
A topic covered in Week 2, focusing on the Vector Space Model for document representation and retrieval.

Information Retrieval Techniques Course
A course covering various topics in information retrieval, including Boolean models, vector space models, recommender systems, and large language models for ranking, with a structured schedule of assignments, quizzes, and a group project, and specific academic policies.

data.entity_name: Information Retrieval Techniques Course Zero Grade Policy
data.content:
Information Retrieval Techniques Course Zero Grade Policy
A policy stating that an assignment will receive a grade of 0.

Information Retrieval Techniques Course TF-IDF
A topic discussing Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency, covered in Week 2.

CSCE 670
CSCE 670 is a course titled "Information Storage and Retrieval" offered in Spring 2026, section 600 (46627), in College Station. It carries 3 credit hours.

CSCE 670 Meeting Schedule
The CSCE 670 course meets as a lecture on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays from 1:50 PM to 2:40 PM at HRBB 113, from January 12, 2026, to May 5, 2026.

Yu Zhang
Yu Zhang is the instructor for CSCE 670. Yu Zhang's email is yuzhang@tamu.edu, office location is PETR 222, and phone number is (217) 954-3457.

Yu Zhang Office Hours
Yu Zhang holds office hours on Mondays from 3:30 PM to 4:30 PM.

College Station
College Station is the campus location where the CSCE 670 course is offered.

HRBB 113
The physical location where the Final Exam will be administered.<SEP>HRBB 113 is the designated meeting location for CSCE 670 lectures.<SEP>The location where the comprehensive final exam will be held.

PETR 222
PETR 222 is the office location for instructor Yu Zhang.

CSCE 310
CSCE 310 is a course that serves as a prerequisite for CSCE 670.

CSCE 603
CSCE 603 is a course that serves as a prerequisite for CSCE 670.

Instructor Approval
Approval of the instructor is an alternative prerequisite for enrollment in CSCE 670.

Graduate Classification
Graduate classification is a required prerequisite for enrollment in CSCE 670.

data.entity_name: CSCE 670 Information Storage And Retrieval Core Concepts
data.content:
CSCE 670 Information Storage And Retrieval Core Concepts
This core topic of CSCE 670 covers the representation, storage, and access to very large multimedia document collections.

data.entity_name: CSCE 670 Web Search Concepts And Models
data.content:
CSCE 670 Web Search Concepts And Models
This topic in CSCE 670 covers key concepts for web search, such as text indexing, retrieval models, evaluation, Web crawling, link-based algorithms (e.g., PageRank), and learning to rank.

data.entity_name: CSCE 670 Fundamental Data Structures And Algorithms
data.content:
CSCE 670 Fundamental Data Structures And Algorithms
This topic covers the fundamental data structures and algorithms essential for information storage and retrieval systems in CSCE 670.

data.entity_name: CSCE 670 Retrieval Systems Design And Evaluation
data.content:
CSCE 670 Retrieval Systems Design And Evaluation
This topic in CSCE 670 includes techniques for designing and evaluating complete retrieval systems, along with algorithms for indexing, compressing, and querying large collections.

data.entity_name: CSCE 670 Recommender Systems Concepts And Models
data.content:
CSCE 670 Recommender Systems Concepts And Models
This topic in CSCE 670 covers key concepts for recommender systems, such as collaborative filtering, matrix factorization, evaluation, and implicit recommendation.

data.entity_name: CSCE 670 Algorithm Design Implementation And Evaluation
data.content:
CSCE 670 Algorithm Design Implementation And Evaluation
This topic focuses on designing, implementing, and evaluating core algorithms for web search and recommendation systems as a learning objective of CSCE 670.

CSCE 670 Recent Research Application
This topic involves identifying salient features and applying recent research results in web search and recommender systems, including adversarial information retrieval, as taught in CSCE 670.

data.entity_name: CSCE 670 Define Recommender Systems Concepts And Models Outcome
data.content:
CSCE 670 Define Recommender Systems Concepts And Models Outcome
Upon completing CSCE 670, learners will be able to define and explain key concepts and models relevant to recommender systems.

data.entity_name: CSCE 670 Define Web Search Concepts And Models Outcome
data.content:
CSCE 670 Define Web Search Concepts And Models Outcome
Upon completing CSCE 670, learners will be able to define and explain key concepts and models relevant to web search.

Recommender Systems
A topic covering systems designed to predict user preferences, including Content-Based and Collaborative Filtering methods, covered in Week 6 of the course.<SEP>Recommender Systems is a key concept that students will learn to define and explain, including various sub-topics.

Learning To Rank
Learning To Rank is a topic covered in the course curriculum.

PageRank
PageRank is an algorithm that students learn about, specifically related to ranking.

Collaborative Filtering
Collaborative Filtering is a topic relevant to recommender systems.

Matrix Factorization
Matrix Factorization is a topic relevant to recommender systems.

Recommender System Evaluation
Recommender System Evaluation is a topic relevant to recommender systems.

Web Search System Algorithms
Web Search System Algorithms are core algorithms students will learn to design, implement, and evaluate for a fully functional web search system.

Implicit Recommendation
Implicit Recommendation is a topic relevant to recommender systems.

Special Course Designation
This section indicates if the course has any special designations, and for this course, it is 'None'.

data.entity_name: Neural Models Of Retrieval And Recommendation
data.content:
Neural Models Of Retrieval And Recommendation
Neural models of retrieval and recommendation are recent research results in web search and recommender systems that students will learn to identify and apply.

Adversarial Information Retrieval
Adversarial Information Retrieval is a recent research result in web search and recommender systems that students will learn to identify and apply.

Mining Of Massive Datasets
An optional textbook authored by Jure Leskovec, Anand Rajaraman, and Jeffrey D. Ullman, published in 2014 and available online.

Jure Leskovec
Co-author of the optional textbook "Mining of Massive Datasets."

Introduction To Information Retrieval
An optional textbook authored by Christopher D. Manning, Prabhakar Raghavan, and Hinrich Schutze, published in 2009 and available online.

Anand Rajaraman
Co-author of the optional textbook "Mining of Massive Datasets."

Prabhakar Raghavan
Co-author of the optional textbook "Introduction to Information Retrieval."

Jeffrey D. Ullman
Co-author of the optional textbook "Mining of Massive Datasets."

Christopher D. Manning
Co-author of the optional textbook "Introduction to Information Retrieval."

Hinrich Schutze
Co-author of the optional textbook "Introduction to Information Retrieval."

Course Grading Scale
The grading scale defines the percentage ranges for letter grades A, B, C, D, and F.

Overall Course Grade
The final grade for the course, determined by combining scores from Homework, Quizzes, Final Exam, and Group Project.

A Grade Threshold
Represents a grade of 90% or higher.

D Grade Threshold
Represents a grade of 60% or higher but less than 70%.

C Grade Threshold
Represents a grade of 70% or higher but less than 80%.

F Grade Threshold
Represents a grade of less than 60%.

B Grade Threshold
Represents a grade of 80% or higher but less than 90%.

Homework
Comprises five programming assignments and contributes 30% to the overall course grade.

Five Programming Assignments
Consist of five individual programming tasks, making up the Homework grade component.

Python
The programming language in which all five programming assignments will be completed.

Final
The final assessment component of the course, contributing 30% to the overall course grade.

Four Brief In-Class Quizzes
These are brief assessments conducted during class, contributing to the Quizzes grade component.

Jupyter Notebook
A tool used for the first programming assignment to help students familiarize themselves with its environment and Python operations.

Comprehensive Final Exam
A comprehensive examination scheduled from 3:30 PM to 5:30 PM on May 4 in HRBB 113, contributing 30% to the overall grade.

Quizzes
Quizzes contribute 5% each to the final grade.<SEP>Comprises four brief in-class quizzes and contributes 20% to the overall course grade.

May 4 Final Exam Schedule
The scheduled date and time for the comprehensive final exam: 3:30 - 5:30 PM on May 4.

Group Project
A mini-research project where students work in teams of 3 or 4, contributing 20% to the final grade.<SEP>A mini-research project undertaken by students in teams of 3 or 4, contributing 20% to the overall course grade.

Homework Collaboration Policy
A policy stating that homeworks are individual assignments, prohibiting sharing code or pair programming, but encouraging discussion of approaches; all help must be acknowledged.<SEP>A policy specifying guidelines for collaboration on homework assignments.

Individual Homework Assignments Policy
A policy stating that homework assignments are individual assignments, prohibiting collaboration.

AI Tools
Tools that can assist in programming tasks, specifically in avoiding syntax errors.

Syntax Errors
Errors in the grammar or structure of programming code that can be avoided.

Generated Code
Code produced by AI tools, which may not be correct and has scope for improvement.

Program Logic Errors
Errors in the underlying logic or functionality of a program, which must be identified through testing.

Comprehensive Documented Testing
A method of rigorous and thorough testing, with recorded steps and results, used to identify errors in program logic.

Mini-Research Project
A project where students work in teams to engage in a mini-research topic, forming the Group Project grade component.

Avoiding Repetitions
A principle for improving generated code by reducing redundant code or tasks.

Separation of Concerns
A design principle for improving generated code by separating program sections into distinct concerns.

Submission
The work submitted by a student, which must adhere to specific standards regarding attribution and validation.

Validation Standards
Rules or guidelines concerning the verification of correctness or authenticity of content within a submission.

Attribution Standards
Rules or guidelines concerning the proper acknowledgment of sources or original work within a submission.

Final Exam
A comprehensive exam contributing 30% to the final grade, scheduled for May 4 from 3:30 PM to 5:30 PM in HRBB 113.

May 4
The date on which the Final Exam is scheduled.

3:30 PM - 5:30 PM
The time slot allocated for the Final Exam.

Homeworks
Academic assignments that are individual, subject to specific collaboration and late policies.

Regrade Policy
A policy allowing students seven days after receiving a graded assignment to request a regrade in writing, with the right to regrade the entire assignment reserved.

Student Rule 7
A student rule concerning excused absences, referenced by the general Late Work Policy.

Late Work Policy
A general policy concerning late submissions, where makeup work for an excused absence is not considered late and is exempt from the policy.

Homework Late Day Policy
A policy specific to homeworks, granting students five total late days (24-hour units), after which late assignments receive a grade of 0.

Course Schedule
A rough, subject-to-change schedule outlining weekly topics for the course.

Course Overview
An introductory topic covered in Week 1 of the course.

Boolean Retrieval
A core topic in information retrieval, covered in Week 1 of the course.

TF-IDF
A term frequency-inverse document frequency weighting scheme, discussed in Week 2 of the course.

BM25
A ranking function used in information retrieval, covered in Week 2 of the course.

Vector Space Model
A model for representing documents and queries as vectors, covered in Week 2 of the course.

Evaluation of Search Engines
A topic focused on methods and metrics for assessing the performance of search engines, covered in Week 4 of the course.

Content-Based Recommender Systems
A type of recommender system that suggests items similar to those a user has previously liked.

Learning to Rank
A machine learning approach for constructing ranking models in information retrieval, covered in Week 5 of the course.

Collaborative Filtering Recommender Systems
A type of recommender system that bases recommendations on the preferences of similar users.

Link Analysis
A method for evaluating web page importance based on their link structure, covered in Week 3 of the course.
